2604.11103.title: ActorMind: Emulating Human Actor Reasoning for Speech Role-Playing
2604.11103.paper_summary: Role-playing has garnered rising attention as it provides a strong foundation for human-machine interaction and facilitates sociological research. However, current work is confined to textual modalities, neglecting speech, which plays a predominant role in daily life, thus limiting genuine role-playing. To bridge this gap, we conceptualize and benchmark speech role-playing through ActorMindBench, and we present a corresponding reasoning framework, called ActorMind. Specifically, (1) Speech Role-Playing enables models to deliver spontaneous responses with personalized verbal traits based on their role, the scene, and spoken dialogue. (2) ActorMindBench is a hierarchical benchmark comprises Utterance-Level content with 7,653 utterances, Scene-Level content with 313 scenes, and Role-Level content with 6 roles. (3) ActorMind is an off-the-shelf, multi-agent, chain-of-though style reasoning framework that emulates how human actors perform in theaters. Concretely, ActorMind first reads its assigned role description via Eye Agent, then comprehends emotional cues within contextual spoken dialogues through Ear Agent. Subsequently, Brain Agent generates a descriptive emotional state, and finally, Mouth Agent delivers the scripts infused with corresponding emotion state. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of ActorMind in enhancing speech role-playing.
2604.11103.paper_summary_zh: 角色扮演引起了越來越多的關注，因為它為人機互動提供了堅實的基礎，並促進了社會學研究。<lb/>然而，目前的工作僅限於文本模式，忽視了在日常生活中扮演主導角色的語音，從而限制了真正的角色扮演。<lb/>為了填補這一空白，我們通過ActorMindBench概念化並基準化語音角色扮演，並提出了一個相應的推理框架，稱為ActorMind。<lb/>具體而言，(1) 語音角色扮演使模型能夠根據其角色、場景和口語對話提供自發的反應，並具備個性化的語言特徵。<lb/>(2) ActorMindBench是一個分層基準，包含7,653個話語層級內容、313個場景層級內容和6個角色層級內容。<lb/>(3) ActorMind是一個現成的多代理鏈式思考風格推理框架，模擬人類演員在劇院中的表現。<lb/>具體來說，ActorMind首先通過Eye Agent閱讀其指定的角色描述，然後通過Ear Agent理解上下文口語對話中的情感線索。<lb/>隨後，Brain Agent生成描述性的情感狀態，最後，Mouth Agent傳遞融合了相應情感狀態的劇本。<lb/>實驗結果證明了ActorMind在增強語音角色扮演方面的有效性。
2604.11103.author: Xi Chen et.al.
2604.11103.authors: Xi Chen, Wei Xue, Yike Guo
